description: Ghidra MCP Server — 225 MCP tools exposing Ghidra's analysis capabilities to AI clients (Claude, Cursor, etc.) via a Python bridge. Requires manual GUI step in Ghidra after install — see notes.

script: if (-not (Test-Path $ghidraExtensions)) { throw "Ghidra extensions directory not found at $ghidraExtensions — is ghidra installed via scoop?" }
Write-Host '' ; Write-Host 'GhidraMCP plugin staged. Manual steps required:' -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  1. Launch Ghidra (any open instance must be restarted)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host "  2. File -> Install Extensions -> tick 'GhidraMCP' -> OK -> restart Ghidra" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host "  3. Open a CodeBrowser, then File -> Configure -> Configure All Plugins -> enable GhidraMCP" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host "  4. Tools -> GhidraMCP -> Start MCP Server  (default port 8089)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host '' ; Write-Host 'Then add to your MCP client config:' -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host 'Note: Ghidra extracted the plugin into your user profile on first launch.' -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host '      To fully remove, delete the GhidraMCP folder under:' -ForegroundColor Yellow

notes: After install, complete the manual Ghidra steps printed above.
Bridge script lives at: $(scoop prefix ghidra-mcp)\bridge_mcp_ghidra.py
Recommended invocation (per upstream README): uv run --script <bridge path>
Plugin is version-pinned to Ghidra 12.0.4 — if scoop bumps Ghidra past what bethington supports, the GUI 'Install Extensions' step will reject it.
